taxonomy: Disterepta pilosa is a species of sea slug, specifically a type of deep-sea gastropod mollusk belonging to the family Clathrodini. These sea slugs are characterized by their unique appearance and are found in deep marine environments around the world. Disterepta pilosa is a part of the phylum Mollusca, class Gastropoda, and is a type of nudibranch. The family Clathrodini is a subfamily within the order Nudibranchia. Further classification of Disterepta pilosa involves its genus, which is part of the family Clathrodini.
